title: internet_outage
description: The organization loses all internet connectivity, impacting external communications and cloud services.

timeline.title: Internet Connectivity Lost
timeline.description: All external connectivity drops simultaneously. External websites and cloud services become unreachable.

timeline.title: Internal Network Verified
timeline.description: Internal LAN is confirmed operational. The outage is isolated to the internet uplink.

timeline.title: ISP Contacted
timeline.description: The ISP is contacted and confirms a network issue on their end affecting the circuit.

timeline.title: Failover Assessment
timeline.description: Engineers assess whether a backup internet circuit or LTE failover is available.

timeline.title: Failover Activated
timeline.description: Backup internet circuit is activated and routing is updated to use the secondary uplink.

timeline.title: Internet Connectivity Restored
timeline.description: All internet connectivity is restored via the backup circuit. ISP continues primary circuit repair.

node-1.title: Complete Internet Outage
node-1.situation: All external internet connectivity has dropped simultaneously. Internal network is functioning. Users cannot reach cloud services, external APIs, or the internet. The organization relies on internet connectivity for cloud-hosted business systems.
node-1.question: What is your immediate action?

options.label: A
options.text: Verify the internal network is intact, check the border router and ISP uplink for connectivity, contact the ISP to report the outage and get an ETA, and notify stakeholders.

options.label: B
options.text: Reboot the core router without first diagnosing whether the problem is internal or with the ISP.

options.label: C
options.text: Wait 30 minutes assuming the ISP will resolve the issue automatically before taking action.

node-2.title: ISP Engagement and Failover
node-2.situation: The ISP confirms a fiber cut affecting your circuit with an estimated repair time of 4 hours. Your organization has a backup LTE failover router but it has not been tested in 6 months. You also have a secondary ISP circuit with limited bandwidth (100 Mbps vs primary 1 Gbps).
node-2.question: How do you proceed?

options.label: A
options.text: Activate the secondary ISP circuit first as the more reliable option, update BGP or static routes to use it, and test connectivity before communicating restored access to users.

options.label: B
options.text: Attempt the untested LTE failover router first, which has unknown reliability and limited capacity.

node-3.title: Bandwidth Management
node-3.situation: The secondary circuit is active and internet connectivity is restored. However, the 100 Mbps secondary circuit is significantly less than the normal 1 Gbps. High bandwidth activities are saturating the link. You need to manage bandwidth to prioritize critical traffic.
node-3.question: What do you do?

options.label: A
options.text: Implement QoS policies on the firewall to prioritize business-critical traffic (VoIP, cloud business apps) and throttle or block non-essential traffic (streaming, large file transfers) until the primary circuit is restored.

options.label: B
options.text: Allow all traffic equally and accept that the secondary circuit will be saturated until the primary is restored.

node-4.title: Recovery Monitoring and Planning
node-4.situation: QoS is managing the bandwidth effectively. Critical services are running on the secondary circuit. The ISP has updated the repair ETA to 2 hours. You need to plan for the primary circuit restoration and assess lessons learned.
node-4.question: What is your next step?

options.label: A
options.text: Monitor the ISP repair progress, prepare to switch back to the primary circuit with minimal disruption when restored, and document the incident with a recommendation to test failover circuits quarterly.

options.label: B
options.text: Immediately switch back to the primary circuit as soon as the ISP says it is restored without validating connectivity first.

node-5.title: Scenario Complete
node-5.situation: You diagnosed the internet outage quickly, activated the secondary ISP circuit as a reliable failover, implemented QoS to prioritize critical traffic on the constrained link, and identified a gap in failover testing procedures.
node-5.question: Scenario complete. Well done!
